meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Scale Your SaaS Projects
content.seo_description: Stop building apps in the dark. Learn how to implement advanced analytics to track user behavior and graduate from basic projects to professional SaaS.
content.answer_block: Moving beyond basic apps requires data-driven feedback loops. By integrating event-based analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform static code into a reactive product. This setup bridges the gap between 'student project' and 'market-ready SaaS' by providing the actionable insights needed to iterate, optimize, and prove your product's value.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks coding, debugging, and styling, yet your app remains a ghost town. The transition from a student building code snippets to an engineer shipping products isn't just about syntax—it's about visibility. Without telemetry, you are flying blind, unable to discern why users drop off or which features actually provide value.

Today, we stop guessing. We are going to wire up a professional-grade analytics pipeline that gives you real-time visibility into user behavior. Using Antigravity for infrastructure and Claude Code for rapid implementation, we will turn your next project into a data-rich environment that proves your worth to future employers.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the key actions that define success in your app. Are you tracking sign-ups, feature usage, or retention? We will map these to event triggers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Engineer. Design a schema for tracking user engagement in a SaaS app. Define 5 core events (e.g., signup_completed, feature_x_used) that correlate with user retention.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --context 'Define event tracking schema in constants/analytics.ts'

steps.title: Initialize the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Integrate your analytics SDK. We focus on a modular architecture so you can swap providers later without refactoring your entire codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a provider-agnostic analytics wrapper class in TypeScript. It should have a trackEvent(name: string, props: object) method.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create src/lib/analytics.ts with a singleton wrapper for event tracking'

steps.title: Implement Middleware Tracking
steps.description: Automate data collection by intercepting route changes or API calls rather than manually adding tracking code to every button.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Next.js middleware or React hook that tracks page views automatically whenever the URL changes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement page view tracking inside the router context'

steps.title: Setup User Identity Context
steps.description: Link anonymous events to specific users. This allows you to see the user journey from landing page to conversion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a function to identify users upon login, passing their ID and email to the analytics provider while preserving privacy.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add identifyUser function to analytics lib and call it after authentication'

steps.title: Debug and Validate the Stream
steps.description: Verify that your data is firing correctly before deploying to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a diagnostic test suite that simulates user events and verifies they are sent to the analytics endpoint correctly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Run integration tests for analytics event firing'

pro_tips: Never track PII (Personally Identifiable Information). Always hash emails before sending them to third-party analytics.
Use 'feature flags' to toggle analytics on/off in development environments to avoid polluting your production data.
Focus on 'Retention' over 'Acquisition' metrics; showing you understand churn makes you 10x more hireable.
Batch your events locally before sending them to the server to reduce API overhead and latency.
Set up an automated alert for 'Event Spikes'—this helps you detect broken features immediately.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, spend 48 hours observing the data. Look for the 'drop-off' point in your user flow and use that as your primary target for your next feature sprint.
content.conversion_block: You now have the visibility that 90% of junior developers lack. You aren't just writing code; you are building a business asset. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
